findings.evidence: `rg -n "prodMulEquiv|piMulEquiv"` shows `@[expose] def` at lines 53 and 86, with `prodMulEquiv_apply`, `prodMulEquiv_symm_apply`, `piMulEquiv_apply`, and `piMulEquiv_symm_apply` at lines 67, 75, 95, and 101.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from both definitions; if any consumer needs more than the existing apply/symm_apply lemmas, add that lemma instead of exposing the body.
findings.issue: `FundamentalGroup.prodMulEquiv` and `FundamentalGroup.piMulEquiv` are marked `@[expose]`, exposing their bodies even though their forward and inverse behavior is already provided by public simp lemmas.

findings.evidence: `rg` finds only the two torus defs at lines 41 and 50; the underlying circle computation has characterization lemmas `fundamentalGroupMulEquiv_zero_apply_eq_iff`, `..._symm_monodromy`, and `..._eq_one_iff` at lines 249, 258, and 267.
findings.fix: Add characteristic lemmas, marked `[simp]` where they are normal forms, for the binary first/second projections and the indexed coordinate of the forward map, plus inverse/monodromy characterizations matching the circle API.
findings.issue: `AddCircle.prodFundamentalGroupMulEquiv` and `AddCircle.piFundamentalGroupMulEquiv` are public equivalences but have no API describing their coordinates or inverse, so users must unfold composed `MulEquiv.trans` definitions to use the torus computation.

summary: The product equivalences expose implementation bodies unnecessarily, and the torus-facing equivalences are published without the characterization lemmas consumers need to use them without unfolding.
